PAPER · 2026-05
data as of 2026-05
CIO — firm conclusion
BOTTOM LINE: trade the gaps between the lenses, harvest breadth, refuse crowds and lone-ARM heroics. TOP ACTIONABLES: (1) VEDL -- firm-wide hold-underweight, do-not-add across all desks until flow reverses; flow (-13,051cr) is the leading signal over ARM 70. (2) LT -- sanctioned active underweight to mandate (Q4 fade), capped by each desk's TE budget, deepest in thematic Infra. (3) BHARTIARTL -- hold CORE on +Rs8,521cr flow + rising ARM + FII; NO overweight in concentrated/Focused books until the optical-PAT delta is confirmed. (4) LICI -- wait for flow; benchmark-weight everywhere except a tiny starter in the Value contra book. (5) Cap cross-desk SOLAR concentration (Premier/Waaree/Solar Inds) at the firm level; Mid Cap correctly declines, Infra holds capped. (6) OVERWEIGHT the Core Equity breadth engine (Multicap/Flexi) and the Healthcare confirm-breadth (Pharma desk); express LT/WIPRO/VEDL as the firm's coordinated underweights. Every overweight must be a triple-confirmed Q1 name sized as breadth -- the portfolio is the edge, no single name is.

← all schemes
Digital firms & schemes per-AMC paper-trading firms — pick a firm, then click a scheme for its scorecard, fact sheet & blotter
A digital firm = one paper book per distinct equity/hybrid scheme of that AMC, each run by the deterministic rules-FM under its own SEBI mandate + liquidity caps (paper-money, no look-ahead, free — no LLM). Firm AUM = Σ each scheme's real AUM. Beat bench · paper = desks whose paper-track CAGR exceeds their benchmark TR since 2015.
★ Read the historical track honestly: a true SECTOR/THEME fund (Banking, Pharma, Digital, Consumption, Transport, Manufacturing, Infra) is now theme-fenced — its 2015→ replay picks ONLY from its own sector(s), and is scored against its sector TR index (NIFTY Healthcare / Financial Services / India Manufacturing …), which removes the broad-market sector beta that made every thematic desk look alike. A residual high IR on some desks (e.g. Digital, Transport) is NOT certified pure stock-selection alpha — the rules-FM is NOT cap-weight-constrained, so within the sector it tilts to smid + momentum names that beat the cap-weighted sector index; that is a factor tilt vs the index, read it as such (Banking, IR ~0.45, is the honest baseline). Diversified mandates (Large/Flexi/Value/Multi-Cap/Small…) select from the broad market vs their broad benchmark, as they should. Characteristic themes (PSU/MNC/ESG/Business-Cycle) are defined by ownership/style, not a sector — with no point-in-time eligibility flag they stay on the broad universe vs NIFTY 500, so their excess still carries some style beta (flagged, a known limitation). The current fact sheet / holdings (the seam book) IS each scheme's real disclosed portfolio — theme-faithful.

book − real CAGR · real — over —y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.20 — the book edges its benchmark: it earned +1.53% excess CAGR per 7.6% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.77 — about 76% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.45 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +6.26% excess CAGR per 13.8% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.073 (t 4.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.78 — about 78% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Banking and Financial Services Fund - Direct Plan - Growth — the paper book ran 5.36% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.59 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.69% excess CAGR per 16.4% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.45 — about 45% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Business Cycle Fund-Direct Growth — the paper book ran 5.55% ahead of the actual fund over 4.5 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.59 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.70% excess CAGR per 16.4% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.45 — about 45% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Conglomerate Fund-Direct Growth — the paper book ran 17.52% behind the actual fund over 1.5 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.08 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +16.98% excess CAGR per 15.7% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.065 (t 6.2) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.56 — about 56% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Consumption Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 15.58% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.43 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +22.78% excess CAGR per 16.0% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.071 (t 6.4) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.62 — about 62% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Digital India Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 17.70% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.43 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +14.81% excess CAGR per 10.3% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.034 (t 6.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.45 — about 44% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Aditya Birla SL ELSS Tax Saver Fund (G)

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.60 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +8.08% excess CAGR per 13.5% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.042 (t 4.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.38 — about 38% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 9.63% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

book − real CAGR · real — over —y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.57 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +4.28% excess CAGR per 7.5% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.77 — about 77% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR -0.39 — the book lags its benchmark: it earned -4.02% excess CAGR per 10.2% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.76 — about 76% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.62 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.87% excess CAGR per 15.8% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.54 — about 54% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life ESG Integration Strategy Fund-Direct Plan-Growth — the paper book ran 11.58% ahead of the actual fund over 5.5 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.47 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +7.37% excess CAGR per 15.8% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.042 (t 4.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.41 — about 41% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Focused Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 7.46% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.35 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +18.66% excess CAGR per 13.9% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 4.4) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.71 — about 71% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Infrastructure Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 16.22% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.62 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +6.19% excess CAGR per 10.0% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.037 (t 3.1) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.82 — about 82% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Large & Mid Cap Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 8.53% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Aditya Birla SL Large Cap Fund (G)

— Book NAV 435   - - NIFTY 100 328 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.28 — the book edges its benchmark: it earned +2.80% excess CAGR per 10.1% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.037 (t 2.4) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.89 — about 89% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Large & Mid Cap Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 2.22% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.67 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +19.16% excess CAGR per 11.4% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.054 (t 5.3) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.69 — about 69% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Manufacturing Equity Fund - Direct Plan - Growth — the paper book ran 19.12% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.46 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +3.68% excess CAGR per 8.0% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.037 (t 2.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.87 — about 87% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Midcap Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 5.43% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Aditya Birla SL MNC Fund - (G)

Aditya Birla Sun Life Mutual Fund · Sectoral / Thematic · benchmark NIFTY 500 · 41 holdings
NAV vs benchmark paper-trade book NAV overlaid on its benchmark TR, rebased to 100 at inception
— Book NAV 790   - - NIFTY 500 366 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.48 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +7.84% excess CAGR per 16.3% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.40 — about 40% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life MNC Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 11.09% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

book − real CAGR · real — over —y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.21 — the book edges its benchmark: it earned +1.62% excess CAGR per 7.7% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.76 — about 76% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Aditya Birla SL Multi-Cap Fund (G)

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.77 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +10.49% excess CAGR per 13.7% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.042 (t 4.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.41 — about 40% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Multi-Cap Fund-Direct Growth — the paper book ran 7.25% ahead of the actual fund over 5.1 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.98 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +10.07% excess CAGR per 10.2% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.043 (t 2.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.84 — about 84% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Pharma and Healthcare Fund-Direct-Growth — the paper book ran 9.09% ahead of the actual fund over 7.0 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.33 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +5.33% excess CAGR per 16.0% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.37 — about 37% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life PSU Equity Fund-Direct Plan-Growth — the paper book ran 0.73% behind the actual fund over 6.5 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.55 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +8.91% excess CAGR per 16.3% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.44 — about 44% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Quant Fund - Direct Growth — the paper book ran 5.66% behind the actual fund over 2.0 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Aditya Birla SL Small Cap Fund (G)

Aditya Birla Sun Life Mutual Fund · Small Cap Fund · benchmark NIFTY SMALLCAP 250 · 60 holdings
NAV vs benchmark paper-trade book NAV overlaid on its benchmark TR, rebased to 100 at inception
— Book NAV 856   - - NIFTY SMALLCAP 250 449 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.03 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +6.65% excess CAGR per 6.4% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.045 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.61 — about 61% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Small Cap Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 6.80% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Aditya Birla SL Special Opportunities Fund (G)

Aditya Birla Sun Life Mutual Fund · Sectoral / Thematic · benchmark NIFTY 500 · 47 holdings
NAV vs benchmark paper-trade book NAV overlaid on its benchmark TR, rebased to 100 at inception
— Book NAV 951   - - NIFTY 500 366 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.61 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.81% excess CAGR per 16.2% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.49 — about 49% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Special Opportunities Fund-Direct-Growth — the paper book ran 6.51% ahead of the actual fund over 5.7 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

book − real CAGR · real — over —y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.39 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +19.05% excess CAGR per 13.8% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.058 (t 5.8) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.73 — about 73% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.20 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +12.04% excess CAGR per 10.1% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.034 (t 6.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.36 — about 36% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Value Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 11.58% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.24 — the book edges its benchmark: it earned +2.32% excess CAGR per 9.8% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.037 (t 2.4) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.82 — about 82% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund (erstwhile Bluechip Fund) - Direct Plan - Growth — the paper book ran 0.67% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.92 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +5.56% excess CAGR per 6.0% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.045 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.43 — about 43% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real quant Small Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 0.50% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.34 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +2.43% excess CAGR per 7.1% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.61 — about 61% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-Forward — the LLM fund managers take the seat as of 2026-06-25 · from the seam on, the agents decide; a deterministic guardrail enforces the mandate
The historical track was built by the deterministic rules-FM. From the latest data date forward, the LLM fund-manager agents read their desk (the inherited book + a candidate universe with validated signals + the rules-FM target as a reference) and set a genuine conviction target book; a deterministic guardrail then clips to the mandate / liquidity / equity-floor. Every bet is pre-registered with a falsifier (anti-hindsight), it is paper-money only with no look-ahead, and the committed audit trail carries no licensed analyst values. Skill will be scored — once next month's data lands — by whether the pre-registered theses played out, via the Fundamental Law (IC·√breadth·TC).
CIO — firm review of this round
House view this round is a single, firm-wide, pro-cyclical value-plus-revisions bet dressed in 27 mandates. Almost every FM said the same thing in their own words: price-momentum and valuation are cross-sectionally flat, so analyst-revision momentum (ARM) is the only signal carrying dispersion — and ARM is currently pointing at cheap energy/PSU (ONGC, Coal India, Chennai Petro), metals (NMDC, Hindalco, National Aluminium, Vedanta), gold/NBFC financials (LICI, Muthoot), and a thin quality-revision sleeve (Grasim, Coforge, Gland, Asian Paints). The result is a coherent and individually-defensible book at each desk but a dangerously consensual one at the firm level. The discipline on display is genuinely good — every FM treats ARM as a small ~1-6mo IC~0.05 tilt rather than a thesis, refuses the illiquid micro-cap ARM-100 tail and the thin-data momentum mirages (NIRLON, HAWKINS, STLNETWORK, BUILDPRO, silver ETFs), collapses the no-signal Vedanta demerger stubs, and several deliberately dial beta down from 1.1-1.17 toward 1.0. So I am not worried about manufactured alpha or curve-fitting. I AM worried that the firm has one bet, not twenty-seven: the same ARM ranking + the same deep-value-cyclical regime read drove the diversified, value, thematic, hybrid and even ESG/MNC desks to overlapping rosters, which collapses effective breadth (the √BR term in IR = IC·√BR·TC). If the commodity/PSU revision up-cycle stalls, the whole firm draws down together; the schemes will not diversify each other.
⚠ Risk flags
  • CROWDING / shared-name concentration is the #1 firm risk: ONGC is a top target in ~14 of 27 books (incl. Large Cap, Flexi, ELSS, BAF, Value, Multi-Asset, ESG, Equity Savings, every relevant thematic) and a holding in ~17; Grasim and NMDC each in ~14; LICI ~13; Vedanta ~12; Muthoot, Hindalco, Coforge each ~10-11. A single firm-aggregate look-through would likely show energy+metals+PSU-financials as the largest house exposure by far — the opposite of what a 27-scheme platform should net to.
  • EFFECTIVE BREADTH COLLAPSE (the √BR leak): the books are not de-correlated. ARM being the only live signal made it a common factor — diversified desks (Flexi, Multi-Cap, ELSS), value desks (Value, Dividend Yield, BAF), and thematics (Business Cycle, Special Opp, ESG, MNC, Quant, Conglomerate) all landed on the same ARM-led cyclical core. The firm is effectively running ~5-6 independent bets, not 27. Cross-scheme correlation of active returns will be high; a regime shock hits every book at once.
  • SINGLE-FACTOR / SINGLE-SITUATION fragility: the whole house leans on one noisy signal (StarMine ARM, IC~0.05, 1-6mo half-life) AND one macro read (commodity/PSU/energy revisions keep rising). Both can turn together — ARM revisions reverse fast, and energy/metals are the most macro-sensitive cohort (oil, China demand, USD). The firm has correlated its alpha source and its macro bet onto the same names.
  • VEDANTA single-issuer concentration: ~12 books carry VEDL as a top target; many explicitly collapsed the 4-5 demerger stubs INTO VEDL (correct individually) but that re-concentrates the whole firm into one corporate family across a dozen schemes. Look through to issuer, not ticker.
  • THEMATIC MANDATE-DRIFT cluster: the momentum thematics (Consumption, Transport & Logistics, Manufacturing, Infrastructure, Digital India, even Midcap) converged on an identical narrow capex/defence/auto-tech basket — MTARTECH, STLTECH, GESHIP, ATLANTAELE, SANSERA, CPPLUS, TDPOWERSYS. Each justified it within-theme, but the Consumption fund holding GE Shipping/MTAR/Sterlite-Tech and the Transport fund holding the same is drift toward a shared 'industrials-momentum' fund. These are also higher-beta, lower-liquidity, parabolic-momentum names sized meaningfully — concentration + liquidity risk stacked.
  • BETA / RISK-ON tilt is firm-wide and pro-cyclical at the same time: a majority of books are flagged risk-on with historical betas 1.09-1.17, and the shared overweight is the highest-beta cohort (metals/energy/capex). Even the desks dialing beta down are doing so INTO cyclicals. The hedged/defensive sleeves (Equity Savings was at 65% cash and -0.39 IR, BAF beta ~0.7, Multi-Asset ~0.63 equity) are now ADDING the same cyclical cluster, eroding the firm's defensive ballast just as the risk-on bet peaks.
  • LIQUIDITY at AUM: several large books (ELSS ~14,000cr, Focused ~7,671cr) and the thematics are sizing mid/small momentum names (MTAR, Sterlite Tech, Atlanta Electricals, Chennai Petro) that the FMs themselves flag as thin; firm-aggregate demand on these names across multiple schemes worsens market-impact and crowding-unwind risk.
Cross-scheme breadth
  • MASSIVE AGREEMENT — possibly too much: the firm has a near-unanimous house tilt to cheap cyclicals + rising revisions. ONGC/NMDC/Grasim/LICI/Hindalco/Muthoot/Vedanta/Coforge form a shared spine running through diversified, value, hybrid, multi-asset AND thematic books. Agreement this broad is a crowding signal, not a conviction signal — when 14 of 27 desks independently 'discover' ONGC, the edge is already priced and the unwind is correlated.
  • SHARED ROOT CAUSE: every FM independently reported the same cross-section (flat z_mom, flat z_val, null flow, ARM the only dispersion). That is why the books rhyme — it is not collusion, it is a common signal environment. Healthy to see consistent signal-reading; unhealthy that the platform has no desk positioned for the OTHER side. No FM is structurally short cyclicals or overweight the defensive/quality-compounder cohort the others are trimming (HUL, Britannia, ITC, ULTRACEMCO, Bajaj Finserv are being cut almost everywhere) — so the firm has no internal hedge.
  • WHERE DESKS GENUINELY DIVERGE (the healthy breadth that DOES exist): (a) the BFSI desk is the cleanest outlier — exchanges/SFBs (MCX, BSE, Karnataka Bank, PNB Housing) with almost no overlap with the cyclical core; (b) Pharma & Healthcare is genuinely orthogonal (CDMO/diagnostics — Gland, Acutaas, Granules, Natco), the firm's best diversifier; (c) the Consumption/Transport/Manufacturing/Infra momentum cluster runs a DIFFERENT shared basket (MTAR/STLTECH/GESHIP/Atlanta/Sansera) — so the firm really has TWO crowds, not one: an energy/metals/PSU value crowd and an industrials/capex momentum crowd, plus the BFSI and Pharma outliers.
  • STYLE-vs-MANDATE coherence is mostly intact at the desk level — Focused is genuinely concentrated (top 8 = ~30%, ICICI 5.5%), Small Cap stays breadth-heavy (79 names, no name >1.5%), Dividend Yield leans cash-cows, ELSS/Flexi keep compounder cores. The drift is not within a book, it is ACROSS books all leaning the same macro way.
  • FUNDAMENTAL-LAW READ for the firm: per-desk IC is fine and TC looks respectable (FMs are confirming ARM with a second leg — value/momentum/franchise — and avoiding the long-only-killing micro-cap tail). The firm's problem is squarely the √BR term: correlated desks shrink effective breadth, so the platform's information ratio will be far below the sum of its books. The single highest-value CIO action is to cap firm-aggregate look-through exposure to the energy/metals/PSU cluster and to Vedanta-the-issuer, and to reward the desks (BFSI, Pharma) that are actually de-correlating the firm — that is where the breadth, and the durable IR, comes from.
  • EARLY-ROUND CAVEAT: many books note last_round_review is null (first scored round), so none of this is hindsight-chasing — the convergence is a genuine same-time signal artifact, not performance-chasing. Worth re-checking next round whether the crowding persists once ARM dispersion rotates.
Aditya Birla SL Large Cap Fund (G)43 names · 95.0% deployed · 70 trades · turnover 92.2%
Risk-on, pro-cyclical: overweight metals/energy and power-capex where momentum AND analyst revisions both lead, anchored by a cheap large-cap financials core; underweight weak-revision power/cement laggards and expensive defensives.
This is a NIFTY 100 large-cap book run with a deliberate cyclical/value tilt for this rebalance. The signal set is unusually coherent: the strongest combination of price momentum and rising analyst revisions sits in metals (VEDL, HINDALCO, NMDC, NATIONALUM, SAIL), energy/commodities (ONGC, COAL INDIA) and power/electrification capital goods (CGPOWER, Hitachi/POWERINDIA, GVT&D, POLYCAB) — so I lean into that commodity-and-capex up-cycle while keeping each sector well inside the 35% cap. Against that I hold a diversified core of genuine large-cap financials (ICICI, Axis, SBI, Kotak, BSE, MCX, Cholamandalam) chosen on cheapness plus steadying revisions, a small revision-led tech sleeve (COFORGE, TCS, Tech Mahindra) where price has lagged but analysts are upgrading, and a thin defensive/quality sleeve (Sun Pharma, Apollo, Titan, HUL, Asian Paints) to keep beta from running away — my track-record beta of 1.09 is already a touch high. I cut the names the signals refute: Adani Energy Solutions and UltraTech (both falling revisions and weak/poor momentum) and the no-signal Vedanta sub-shell VAML. ARM is treated as a small 1-6 month tilt (IC~0.05), never a standalone reason to own anything — every overweight needs momentum, valuation, or franchise backing too. No last-round review exists yet, so nothing is re-chased or vindicated on hindsight; this is the first scored book.

Aditya Birla SL Flexi Cap Fund (G)54 names · 95.0% deployed · 77 trades · turnover 53.6%
Risk-aware quality flexi-cap: hold the diversified compounder core, lean modestly toward names with rising analyst revisions, prune the falling-revision laggards, and dial beta down from 1.15.
This round the cross-section is flat on price-momentum and valuation (z_mom and z_val are ~0 for nearly every candidate), so the only signal carrying genuine dispersion is analyst-revision momentum (ARM). ARM is a small ~1-6 month tilt (IC ~0.05), not a thesis on its own, and the quant baseline that ranks straight off it is loaded with illiquid micro-caps on pure revision pops. So I do NOT chase the ARM top. Instead I keep my diversified flexi-cap core of quality franchises and use ARM as a confirming overlay: ADD where strong/rising revisions coincide with a real franchise and reasonable liquidity (Grasim, Lenskart, CreditAccess, Asian Paints, plus selective new large/mid names ONGC, LICI, Coforge, Muthoot), HOLD the steady compounders, and TRIM or EXIT names whose revisions are clearly falling (L&T, HPCL, Adani Ports, SRF, Sumitomo Chem, Max Financial, Sona, OneSource). Net effect: a broadly diversified ~55-name book that respects the 40-70 count band and 35% sector cap, leans gently into improving fundamentals, and pulls beta down from 1.15 toward ~1.0 by cutting the high-beta cyclicals with deteriorating revisions. Paper book; no single signal is sized as a guarantee.

Quality-growth core, modestly de-risked: lean into rising-revision compounders, prune falling-revision laggards, add liquid value-with-revisions large/mids.
This is a diversified ELSS book (benchmark NIFTY 500) carrying a high historical beta (~1.17), so this rebalance trims the tail of names where analyst revisions are clearly falling and conviction is already low, and concentrates into structural compounders and financials where revisions are still rising. Across the candidate set momentum z-scores are uniformly flat (the market is range-bound, no strong cross-sectional trend), so the live edge this round comes from the analyst-revision tilt and valuation, not price momentum. I keep the franchise core (private banks, Bharti, Reliance, Titan, M&M, DMart, NTPC, Cummins, Sun Pharma), add a small sleeve of liquid, cheaper, rising-revision large/mids (ONGC, LICI, Muthoot, Coforge, Grasim, Asian Paints, Gland, NMDC, Hindustan Zinc) and exit fourteen low-revision / low-conviction tails. I deliberately avoid the handful of candidates with extreme brain_score driven by a single blown-out momentum or value z (NIRLON, HAWKINCOOK, STLNETWORK, IT, BUILDPRO) and the sub-100-rupee thin names — those are illiquidity/value-trap artifacts, not real edge for a 14,000-crore book.

Aditya Birla SL Balanced Advantage Fund (G)50 names · 60.0% deployed · 78 trades · turnover 57.3%
Defensive-neutral balanced advantage; value-plus-revision tilt to cheap energy/materials/PSU and selective quality, trimming the low-conviction tail and falling-revision value traps.
As a Balanced Advantage fund benchmarked to NIFTY 500 with a regime-switch brain and a defensive beta near 0.7, I run a neutral-to-defensive equity stance and tilt the equity sleeve toward names where cheapness and rising analyst revisions agree, since that combination has the most durable expected edge in this universe. I overweight cheap energy and materials with strong rising revisions (ONGC, Chennai Petro, NMDC, National Aluminium, Hindalco, Coal India), add high-revision financial compounders (LIC, Muthoot, Grasim) and pockets of quality IT/pharma with rising revisions (Coforge, Gland, Zydus), and keep a diversified spine of core large-cap banks and franchises so no single sector breaches its cap. I deliberately avoid the two traps in this candidate set: cheap-but-falling-revision value traps (PNB, HPCL, IOC, Tata Motors PV) and momentum blow-offs with no valuation or revision support (Sterlite Tech, MTAR). I concentrate from 60 holdings toward roughly 50, cutting the lowest-conviction tail to size my best ideas larger, while staying within the 35-60 holding band and the 35% sector cap.

Aditya Birla SL Focused Fund (G)24 names · 95.0% deployed · 34 trades · turnover 59.2%
Risk-aware quality-at-reasonable-valuation; keep the structural compounders, lean the analyst-revision tailwind only via liquid quality, cut the deep-downgrade cyclical tail and nudge beta down.
This is a focused fund whose edge is concentrated conviction, not breadth. The quant baseline this round is effectively a pure analyst-revision (ARM) chase stacked into thinly-covered small-caps that all read flat on momentum and valuation — exactly the single-signal trap I'm warned against, and a poor fit for a 7,671 cr book on liquidity grounds. So I keep my core of structurally compounding large-caps (private banks, Bharti, Reliance, M&M, Trent) where a soft 1-6 month revision reading is noise against a multi-year franchise thesis; I add a disciplined sleeve of names that carry rising revisions AND independently clear a quality/liquidity/value bar (ONGC, GRASIM, MUTHOOTFIN, LICI, COFORGE) so the ARM tilt is confirmed by a second leg, never alone; and I cut the deep-downgrade, low-conviction cyclical tail (Adani complex, Biocon, Voltas, Whirlpool, Havells, UltraTech, United Spirits) rather than average down. Net effect lowers high-beta cyclical exposure — my live beta of 1.12 means part of past excess return is leverage, not alpha, and I want this rebalance to add selection, not more beta.

Aditya Birla SL Equity Hybrid '95 Fund (G)41 names · 72.5% deployed · 69 trades · turnover 57.4%
Risk-on but quality-anchored: lean into cheap, large/mid names where analyst revisions and momentum agree; fund it by exiting dead-signal placeholder lines and richly-valued, falling-revision laggards.
As an aggressive-hybrid book benchmarked to NIFTY 500 with a defensive history (beta 0.86, IR 0.57), I am deploying part of my 27.5% cash toward the cleaner end of the candidate set while keeping diversification (40-50 names, within the 35-60 band). The edge I am pressing this round is the convergence of three independent validated forces: cross-sectional cheapness (z_val), peer-relative price momentum (z_mom), and rising sell-side analyst revisions (treated as a small 1-6 month tilt, never a guarantee). The desk's own regime-switch brain_score and the quant baseline both point to commodity/energy/PSU value (ONGC, NMDC, NATIONALUM, HINDALCO, COALINDIA) and select revision-led mid-caps (GLAND, GRASIM, MUTHOOTFIN, NYKAA, LENSKART) where revisions are strong AND the name is not expensive. I overweight names where cheapness and rising revisions coincide, hold the diversified core of banks/IT/consumer at benchmark-like weight, and trim or exit two groups: (1) the Vedanta demerger placeholder lines (VAML/VISL/VEDPOWER/VOGL) which carry no usable signal and are not conviction bets, and (2) richly-or-fairly-valued names with weak/falling revisions and poor momentum (GODREJCP, ULTRACEMCO, BAJAJFINSV, PERSISTENT). Flow data is null this round, so I deliberately do not lean on smart-money flow and instead size on the value+momentum+revision triangulation. Net result is a slightly more cyclical, value-tilted book than the current one, still well inside the mandate's spirit.

Aditya Birla SL Multi-Cap Fund (G)68 names · 95.0% deployed · 84 trades · turnover 39.8%
Risk-on but disciplined: a measured analyst-revision (ARM) tilt on a diversified multi-cap core; trim where revisions are collapsing, keep momentum/value as confirmation only since both are flat this round.
This round the cross-section gives me exactly one live edge: analyst-revision momentum (ARM). Price-momentum and valuation z-scores are flat across the liquid universe, and net-fund-flow is unavailable, so I refuse to manufacture conviction from dead signals. I therefore run my existing diversified multi-cap book and apply a moderate ARM tilt: keep and modestly add the names where revisions are strong/rising (Lenskart, Arvind, Coforge, City Union Bank, Hexaware, Bharat Forge, Granules, Groww, Varun Beverages), refresh the book with a handful of liquid large/mid names carrying strong revisions (ONGC, LICI, Muthoot, Grasim, Asian Paints), and cut the names where analysts are actively downgrading and there is no value or momentum offset (HPCL, L&T, RBL, Adani Ports, Sumitomo Chem, JK Cement, Max Financial, Adani Energy, Ramco). I keep liquid large-cap bank anchors for ballast, deliberately avoid loading micro-caps just because their ARM prints 100 (a tiny-IC signal is least reliable and least implementable there at this AUM), and trim the five-line Vedanta demerger cluster to cut single-situation concentration. The book stays inside mandate spirit on sector and name caps and near the top of the holding band, consistent with a diversified Multi Cap mandate; ARM is treated as a small 1-6 month tilt, never a guarantee.

Aditya Birla SL Multi Asset Allocation Fund-Reg (G)43 names · 60.0% deployed · 72 trades · turnover 60.3%
Risk-balanced multi-asset, ~63% equity; overweight quality-value cyclicals (energy, metals, select financials) with firming analyst revisions, underweight crowded value-traps.
As a 40-80% multi-asset fund benchmarked to NIFTY 500 with a historically defensive beta (~0.71), my edge this round is to harvest the quant baseline's deep-value cyclical tilt SELECTIVELY rather than wholesale. The market is offering cheap energy, metals and PSU-adjacent names; I lean into those where cheapness is CONFIRMED by stabilizing-to-rising analyst revisions and non-negative momentum (ONGC, CHENNPETRO, GESHIP, NMDC, NATIONALUM, HINDALCO, COALINDIA, LICI, MUTHOOTFIN), and I refuse the names that are cheap only because analysts are actively cutting estimates and price is rolling over (HINDPETRO, IOC, PNB on raw value) — those are value-traps, not value. I keep a diversified large-cap financial/IT/consumer spine to preserve the fund's lower-beta, all-weather character and to respect the 35% sector cap, but I trim the lowest-conviction laggards where revisions are falling and momentum is weak (HDFCLIFE, GODREJCP, ULTRACEMCO, BAJAJFINSV). I deliberately discount the two extreme momentum z-scores (STLTECH ~+8.9, MTARTECH ~+5.5) as likely illiquid noise, not conviction. ARM is treated as a small 1-6 month tilt, synthesized with momentum, valuation and flows — never as a standalone bet. Net: a ~42-name book that keeps the defensive backbone while adding a measured pro-cyclical, quality-value overweight where the signals genuinely agree.

ONGC cyclical 3.3%
Deep value (top-decile cheapness) confirmed by strengthening revisions and non-negative momentum; oil/gas re-rating leg with a defensive dividend floor suits a multi-asset book.
✗ wrong if: Crude rolls over and analyst revisions turn negative, or ONGC underperforms the broad energy basket over the next quarter despite its cheapness.
CHENNPETRO cyclical 2.2%
Refining-margin cyclical trading at a deep discount where revisions are firming — a mean-reversion bet with analyst support, sized modestly as a mid-cap.
✗ wrong if: Refining cracks compress and revisions roll over, or the stock keeps lagging while peers (ONGC) re-rate — signalling a structural, not cyclical, discount.
ICICIBANK structural 2.2%
Highest-quality large private bank with steady franchise compounding; the spine of the financials sleeve in a lower-beta book.
✗ wrong if: NIMs compress sharply, credit costs rise, and the bank loses share to peers while revisions turn negative.
GESHIP cyclical 2.0%
Shipping-cycle cyclical where price momentum, valuation and analyst revisions agree — highest signal-confluence name in the universe, so I lean in.
✗ wrong if: Freight rates/shipping cycle peak and momentum breaks down, or revisions fade — the confluence that justified the overweight disappears.
NMDC cyclical 1.9%
Metals cyclical with firming analyst revisions and positive relative momentum; a liquid large-cap way to add commodity beta to the book.
✗ wrong if: Iron-ore prices and steel demand weaken, momentum turns negative, and revisions reverse lower.
NATIONALUM cyclical 1.8%
Aluminium cyclical with the best momentum among the metals names plus strengthening revisions — a leveraged play on the base-metals up-leg.
✗ wrong if: LME aluminium rolls over, momentum breaks, and revisions stall — the commodity tailwind reverses.

Aditya Birla SL Value Fund (G)69 names · 95.0% deployed · 86 trades · turnover 62.3%
Risk-aware value, tilting cash into cheap names where analyst revisions are turning up; pruning the refuted, low-conviction tail.
As a NIFTY 500 value fund I want cheapness PLUS a fundamental catalyst, not cheapness alone. The desk's value_revision brain is telling me where the two coincide: a cluster of reasonably-valued energy, financials, materials and select consumer/IT names where analysts are actively upgrading (strong, rising revisions) sits at the top of my candidate ranking, while a tail of my current holds (gas utilities, FMCG, cement, some defensives) now carries clearly falling revisions. With no prior round to grade (last_round_review is null) I have no winners to defend or losers to atone for, so I pre-register a fresh book: deploy the 5% cash and rotate out of the falling-revision tail into improving-revision value, keep the diversified ~55-60 name structure inside the 40-70 band, respect the 9% name / 35% sector spirit (I stay far below both), and treat analyst revisions as a small 1-6 month tilt synthesized WITH valuation, momentum and my existing positioning — never as a standalone bet. I deliberately avoid the handful of optically deep-value tickers with no analyst coverage and negligible blended score (likely value traps / data artifacts) and I keep the Vedanta demerger entities as modest structural holds rather than chase them.

Holdco/cement-and-VSF conglomerate at a holdco discount with improving estimates; structural value as paints/B2B scale.

VEDL structural 2.0%
High-yield diversified resources holdco at a holdco discount, leveraged to commodities and the demerger unlock; positive momentum and value.
✗ wrong if: Commodity downturn or balance-sheet/debt stress at the parent reverses the thesis.
